commit.message:
feat: add Pattern 5 — Orchestrator Self-Governance

The orchestrator (main LLM session) is the hidden mega-agent. It
accumulates every sub-agent result and silently degrades. This pattern
adds three rules: never absorb full output (use file paths), declare
the bus manifest upfront, and budget your own context window.

Discovered during a real session where 24+ research agents returned
results directly to the orchestrator, causing context rot.

Pass base_path output to build step as NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH

configure-pages patches next.config.js directly (only supports .js/.cjs/.mjs,
not .ts) and never sets NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH as an env var. Our image path
code therefore always saw an empty string at build time.

Give the Setup Pages step an id and pipe its base_path output explicitly into
the Build step so process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH is correctly set to
/crispkatbe when next build runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Fiat-Shamir sponge (spongefish) with linear ownership

New nodes: absorb_public, absorb_private, squeeze (+3 ops), Sponge type tag.
Sponge values are linear resources — each can be used exactly once.
absorb returns new sponge, squeeze returns Pair(challenge, new_sponge).
Type dispatch: Field, Curve, Array, Polynomial, MLE, Pair all supported.
7 tests: absorb public/private, squeeze, multi-round, linearity, determinism.
49 nodes, 327 tests.

`.gitHub/` Updates (#4)

* init updates

* Add frontend agent and session hooks (PS & SH)

-Add a new frontend agent (.github/agents/frontendReactDeveloper.md) and reformat the code reviewer agent (.github/agents/codeReviewer.md) to improve checklist structure and headings.
- Introduce PowerShell session hooks for file-size and task-health checks (.github/hooks/*/*.ps1), update the existing shell scripts to also inspect .github paths and backlog files, and register both bash and powershell entries in hooks.json (use "type", "bash", "powershell", "timeoutSec").
- Hooks are non-blocking and warn about large files or stale tasks.

* Standardize prompt frontmatter and prompts README

- Normalize Copilot prompt files and docs: add a unified YAML frontmatter format (description, agent, tools, optional model) and update invocation docs in README (use /<name> or #prompt:<name>).
- Replace legacy keys (name, user-invocable, disable-model-invocation, argument-hint, allowed-tools, etc.) with agent/tools fields, add tool lists and agent modes for prompts, and refine prompt descriptions.
- Files updated: .github/prompts/README.md and individual prompts (agent-browser.md, calibrate.md, code-reviewer.md, create-prd.md, create-workflow.md, evo.md, execute.md, extract-best-practices.md, handle-pr-review.md, plan-feature.md, pre-pr-checklist.md, tasks.md).
- This makes prompt metadata consistent and enables clearer tool/agent capabilities.

* Rename .github/rules to .github/instructions

- Move rule docs from .github/rules/ to .github/instructions/ and add YAML frontmatter with applyTo for each file.
- Update meta-management references from `.claude/rules/` to `.github/instructions/` and adjust the drift message.
- Consolidate testing path patterns into an applyTo string and change the calibration hint from `/calibrate` to `#prompt:calibrate`.

pdf-object: implement ASCII85Decode filter

Add `Filter::ASCII85Decode` variant, map the `"ASCII85Decode"` name in
the `From` impl, and implement `Filter::decode_ascii85` using Horner's
method with wrapping arithmetic. Wire it into `StreamObject::data()`.

Includes 7 unit tests covering full groups, the `z` shorthand, partial
final groups, whitespace skipping, the `~>` EOD marker, and invalid
character errors.

Task 1 - Recalibrate parser core/context confidence composition:

  formula edge cases:
  * Combined core+context penalty: healthField/budget go to core channel,
    bottleneckCoverage goes to context channel — exact numeric verification
  * Floor-break behavior: large context penalty pushes aggregate to 0.1,
    documents when the core-context invariant intentionally breaks
  * Property invariant: parserConfidence is always >= 0.1 across all
    penalty combinations (5 input cases)
  * Channel invariant: holds exactly when floor is not triggered
    (uses 3 bottlenecks, 1 covered → coverage=0.33 < FLOOR=0.5)
  * Core floor: stacked penalties can't push parserCoreConfidence < 0.1
  * Channel routing: requestBudget penalty goes to core (not context)
  * Channel routing: healthField penalty goes to core (not context)

Task 2 - Add governance BLOCK end-to-end flow assertions:
- Add 8 new tests to orchestrator_gate_precedence.test.ts covering
  decision envelope propagation:
  * Budget exhaustion gate: writes temp budget file (remainingUsd=0),
    verifies BUDGET_ELIGIBILITY fires with correct gateIndex
  * Carry-forward debt gate: writes temp ledger with 3 critical overdue
    entries, verifies CARRY_FORWARD_DEBT fires with correct gateIndex
  * Precedence ordering: freeze (idx=3) fires before drift (idx=7)
    when both conditions are simultaneously active
  * All blocked gate types carry non-null reason + numeric gateIndex >= 1
  * cycleId propagation: non-blocked result carries the argument cycleId
  * Evidence coupling reason: encodes the failing plan's task_id
  * Negative path: plan with valid evidence does NOT trigger coupling gate
  * Clean pass envelope: empty plans + disabled gates → all optional
    fields absent (no gateIndex, no mandatoryDriftPaths, etc.)

* feat(prometheus): split parser confidence into core and context-penalty channels

- Add parserContextPenalty: context/external signals (bottleneckCoverage, architectureDrift)
- parserConfidence remains aggregate = max(0.1, parserCoreConfidence - parserContextPenalty) — backward compat

* feat(prometheus): use resolved canonical health in parser confidence scoring

Replace raw field-only presence check with 3-tier resolved canonical health
scoring in normalizePrometheusParsedOutput:

- healthField = 1.0: model provided an explicit canonical or alias value
  (no penalty, unchanged behaviour for valid inputs)
- healthField = 0.9: field was absent/unrecognized but projectHealth was
  successfully resolved via inferProjectHealth (lighter penalty
  health_field_inferred_from_text, delta -0.1 instead of -0.2)
- healthField = 0.8: unresolvable (defensive; currently unreachable because
  inferProjectHealth always returns a canonical default)

This ensures cycles where the LLM omits the projectHealth field but the
analysis text carries a recognisable health signal are not over-penalised.
All 3549 tests pass; zero new lint errors.

commit.message:
test(prometheus): healthField confidence reflects structural quality, not alias variation

- Update healthField component comment to clarify it measures structural
  completeness (canonical or known alias present) vs absent/unrecognizable
- Add inline comment before healthFieldValid computation explaining that
  alias normalization happens before scoring so aliases and canonical values
  score identically (structural quality, not enum alias variation)
- Add 4 tests to 'confidence components' describe block:
  * healthField=1.0 for 'healthy' alias (same as canonical 'good')
  * healthField=1.0 for 'warning' alias (same as canonical 'needs-work')
  * healthField=1.0 for all canonical values ('good','needs-work','critical')
  * healthField=0.8 for unrecognized value — negative path structural gap

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: unify health aliases, strengthen artifact gates, harden forbidden cmds (#131)

Task 1 – Health enum alias mapping
- Add PLANNER_HEALTH_ALIASES and normalizeProjectHealthAlias() to prometheus.ts
  so LLM alias values ('healthy', 'warning') are resolved to canonical
  ('good', 'needs-work') before validation and confidence scoring.
  Alias inputs now score healthField=1.0 instead of 0.8 (inference fallback).
- Apply alias normalization inside computeHealthDivergence() in orchestrator.ts
  so plannerHealth aliases don't produce spurious 'unknown' divergence states.
- Tests: 13 new assertions in orchestrator_health_divergence.test.ts and
  prometheus_parse.test.ts covering alias resolution, case-insensitivity,
  pass-through of canonical values, and negative paths.

Task 2 – Strengthen done-path artifact assertions across role profiles
- Add role-specific artifact gate tests for 'qa', 'security', and 'api'
  profiles in verification_gate.test.ts, including negative paths for
  missing SHA, missing npm output block, and unfilled placeholder tokens.
- Tests: 8 new assertions covering explicit BOX_MERGED_SHA marker preference
  over loose hex detection, explicit NPM test block detection, and per-role
  enforcement of the artifact gate requirement.

Task 3 – Harden forbidden command checks for shell-glob test invocations
- Fix checkForbiddenCommands() in verification_command_registry.ts to
  .trim() input before pattern matching, consistent with rewriteVerification-
  Command(). Leading whitespace previously caused ^-anchored patterns
  (bash, sh, npx tsx, ts-node) to silently miss forbidden invocations.
- Tests: 8 new assertions in verification_command_registry.test.ts and
  plan_contract_validator.test.ts for leading-whitespace variants of all
  forbidden patterns plus a no-false-positive check for canonical commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
